stories.title: NBA Finals Game 2 Preview — Spurs Must Respond or Fall Into 0-2 Hole
stories.angle: Wemby vows "play normal" as Spurs face must-win Game 2 at home; Shaq says attack the paint; Knicks riding 12-game win streak and +5.5 road underdog again

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Game 2. Tonight. 8:30 PM ET on ABC.

The Knicks stole Game 1 in San Antonio. Now the Spurs have their backs against the wall at home.

Wemby says he's "not worried." Brunson says the job isn't done.

Let's find out who's right. 🏀 #Knicks #Spurs #NBAFinals

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Knicks went into San Antonio and took Game 1. Now they're +180 underdogs in Game 2 — despite winning by 10 on the road.

The Spurs were -5.5 favorites in Game 1 too.

Just saying. #NBAFinals #Knicks #Spurs

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
The Spurs were 25.6% from 3 and had 16 assists in Game 1.

That's not Wemby's problem — that's a whole team problem.

San Antonio needs to be San Antonio tonight. Ball movement. Vassell from deep. Wemby in the paint.

8:30 PM on ABC. #Spurs #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

Game 2 of the 2026 NBA Finals is tonight, and the stakes could not be higher for the San Antonio Spurs.

The Knicks pulled off one of the better Game 1 road wins in Finals history — coming back from 14 down in the third quarter to win 105-95, with Jalen Brunson delivering a 30-point performance and 12 fourth-quarter points that swung the series. New York now leads 1-0 and enters Game 2 on a 12-game playoff winning streak.

Tonight the Spurs must respond. Victor Wembanyama has been characteristically calm — "I'm not worried. I just have to play normal" — but San Antonio has real structural issues to fix. They shot just 25.6 percent from three and had only 16 team assists in Game 1. The offense needs to flow.

Shaquille O'Neal put it plainly this week: Wemby has to attack the paint tonight. If he keeps settling for jump shots over long, outstretched arms — especially with fatigue creeping in — the Knicks will keep him in check. The paint is where he changes the game.

Tip-off is at 8:30 PM ET on ABC from Frost Bank Center in San Antonio. The Spurs are favored by 5.5 points. But after what the Knicks did in Game 1, nobody is sleeping on New York.

Who wins tonight? Drop your prediction below! 👇
facebook.image_caption: Game 2. Tonight. 8:30 PM ET. ABC. Frost Bank Center. 🏀 The Spurs need a statement. The Knicks want 2-0. #NBAFinals #Knicks #Spurs

article.headline: Can the Spurs Bounce Back? What Has to Change in Game 2 of the NBA Finals

stories.title: Josh Hart's Larry Bird Moment — 15 Reb, 6 Ast, 4 Stl in Game 1
stories.angle: Hart becomes first player since Larry Bird (1986) to post 15+ reb/6+ ast/4+ stl in a Finals game; Brunson calls him "a kid"; Knicks +22 in his 27 minutes

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Josh Hart's Game 1 line:

15 REB. 6 AST. 4 STL. 1 BLK. 27 minutes.

First player since LARRY BIRD (1986 Finals) to post those numbers in an NBA Finals game.

No. 1 never scored much. No. 1 doesn't need to. #Knicks #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

Josh Hart did something in Game 1 of the NBA Finals that hadn't been done since Larry Bird.

Hart finished with 15 rebounds, six assists, four steals and a block in 27 minutes — scoring just three points on five shots. It is the first time any player has posted 15-plus rebounds, six-plus assists, and four-plus steals in an NBA Finals game since Bird went off for 25 points, 15 rebounds, 11 assists and four steals in Game 3 of the 1986 Finals against the Houston Rockets.

And the Knicks were +22 in Hart's minutes. Not +22 for the game — just in the 27 minutes Hart was on the floor.

When Jalen Brunson was asked about Hart after the game, he called him "a kid." Not as an insult — as the highest possible compliment. Hart plays with the unbothered, relentless energy of someone who has nothing to prove and everything to give.

He corralled Wembanyama's critical late-game turnover and helped drain the clock. He clawed for every loose ball. He guarded whoever the Spurs needed guarded. He made the Knicks +22 in 27 minutes without making a single basket that will show up on a highlight reel.

There's no award for what Josh Hart does. There's no way to explain it to someone who only looks at scoring columns. You just watch and feel it — and last night, the entire city of New York felt it.

The Hart era in New York is real. And it started with 40 years of waiting for a moment like this. 🌟
facebook.image_caption: 3 points. 15 rebounds. 6 assists. 4 steals. Josh Hart in Game 1 of the NBA Finals. First since Larry Bird. 🤯 #Knicks #NBAFinals #JoshHart

article.headline: The Hart of It All: Josh Hart Joins Larry Bird in the NBA Finals History Books

stories.title: Wembanyama's Road Back — What Must Change for the Spurs in Game 2
stories.angle: Wemby 6-21 FG/6 TOV in Game 1; Shaq's prescription: attack the paint; Spurs shot 25.6% from 3/only 16 AST; "I just have to play normal" — MRI cleared limp

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Wembanyama on his Game 1 shooting (6-21): "I just have to play normal."

Shaq agrees — but adds Wemby has to stop settling for jumpers. Attack the paint. Make the Knicks foul him.

Game 2 tonight 8:30 ET. We find out. #Spurs #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

Victor Wembanyama shot 6-of-21 in Game 1. He had six turnovers. He limped at one point and the crowd in San Antonio gasped. An MRI cleared him.

And when reporters asked him about it afterward, Wembanyama was calm.

"I just have to play normal," he said. "I'm not worried. We just need to approach the game with a better mental state."

That's not bravado. That's a 22-year-old who has seen enough adversity in his career to understand that one bad shooting night isn't a career-defining moment. He knows what he is.

But NBA Hall of Famer Shaquille O'Neal had some advice this week that goes a step further: Wemby needs to stop settling for jump shots. His length and footwork are most effective in the paint — not chasing contested threes over Knicks wings who can match his wingspan. Getting to the rim forces the Knicks to foul him. It changes the game.

The Spurs shot only 25.6 percent from three in Game 1 and managed just 16 assists — neither of those numbers is going to beat a Knicks team playing at its current level.

Game 2 is tonight at 8:30 PM ET. We're about to find out whether Wemby's calmness was confidence or denial. Buckle up.
facebook.image_caption: "I just have to play normal." — Victor Wembanyama, after going 6-21 in Game 1. Game 2 tonight at 8:30 PM ET. 🏀 #Spurs #NBAFinals #Wembanyama

article.headline: Wembanyama's Road Back: What 'Playing Normal' Must Look Like in Game 2

stories.title: 2026 NBA Draft — Wizards Win Lottery, Dybantsa Says He's Their Pick
stories.angle: Washington wins No. 1 pick (first since John Wall 2010); BYU's Dybantsa 25.5 PPG/51% FG says he's "100% sure" he's the pick; draft June 23-24 Brooklyn

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Wizards land the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft (June 23, Brooklyn).

AJ Dybantsa — 25.5 PPG, 51% FG at BYU — says he's "100% sure" he's their pick.

First time Washington picks No. 1 since John Wall in 2010.

New era? 👀 #NBADraft2026

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

The 2026 NBA Draft is less than three weeks away — June 23 in Brooklyn — and the Washington Wizards are holding the most important ticket in the building.

Washington won the 2026 NBA Draft Lottery, securing the No. 1 overall pick for the first time since they selected John Wall in 2010. With a 17-65 record and a 14.0 percent chance, the Wizards hit the jackpot.

And the player waiting at the other end of that ticket? AJ Dybantsa.

The 6'9", 210-pound BYU forward averaged 25.5 points per game this season on 51.0 percent shooting — the highest scoring average in Division I basketball. He has length, skill, and scoring ability at every level of the court, and most draft analysts see him as the consensus No. 1 in a historically deep class.

Dybantsa himself? He's not shy about it. "I'm 100 percent sure I'll be the Wizards' pick," he said this week.

There's been a small whisper of uncertainty — Dybantsa mentioned he'd be open to working out for multiple teams — but the overwhelming expectation remains that he lands in Washington.

The complete top five: Wizards (1), Jazz (2), Grizzlies (3), Bulls (4), Clippers — via a Pacers trade (5).

A new era is coming to D.C. Are you ready for it? 🏀
facebook.image_caption: Washington Wizards win the 2026 Draft Lottery. No. 1 pick. AJ Dybantsa incoming? 📋 Draft is June 23 in Brooklyn. #NBADraft2026 #Wizards

article.headline: Washington Wins the Lottery: AJ Dybantsa and the Wizards' New Beginning

stories.title: Giannis Trade Market — Heat Are Now the Frontrunners
stories.angle: Multiple insiders say Heat are frontrunners; Bam only player off limits; Bucks "open for business" per Shams; decision expected near June 23 draft

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
NBA insiders: The Heat are now the frontrunners to land Giannis.

The Bucks are "open for business." Bam Adebayo is the only Miami player off limits.

Decision expected around the June 23 draft. The Giannis era in Milwaukee may be ending.

#Heat #Bucks #NBA

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:30 PM ET

The Giannis Antetokounmpo era in Milwaukee is ending. The only question is where he goes next.

League insiders are now pointing toward Miami. Multiple people around the NBA — agents, executives, sources close to the situation — believe Giannis will end up with the Heat. The Bucks have made clear they are "open for business," and Miami has reportedly made Antetokounmpo their primary target.

The Miami framework: they are willing to offer almost everything except Bam Adebayo, who remains the one untouchable in any trade package.

Portland is also in pursuit, but most insiders are skeptical the Trail Blazers can close the deal. Giannis has a well-documented preference for staying in the Eastern Conference, and Portland is as far west as it gets.

There's a timeline here. Giannis has two years left on his contract — the second is a player option — and he becomes eligible for a four-year, $275 million extension on October 1. A trade that gets done before the June 23 NBA Draft would give a new team the best chance at locking him up long-term before free agency becomes a factor.

The Heat. The Bucks. Bam. Giannis. One of the most fascinating offseason trades in recent memory is being assembled in real time.

Where do you think Giannis ends up? 👇
facebook.image_caption: Heat are the frontrunners to land Giannis. The Bucks are "open for business." The NBA offseason is already wild. 👀 #Giannis #Heat #Bucks #NBA

article.headline: Miami Heat Are the Frontrunners for Giannis as Bucks Trade Market Takes Shape
